Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results states a putative IbHCT with protein ID BAJ14794 was identified and cloned.

Source_Species.reason: IbHCT is described as isolated/cloned from I. batatas, i.e., sweet potato.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly studies IbHCT from Ipomoea batatas.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional assays used the gene from sweet potato; IbHCT was cloned from I. batatas and analyzed as a sweet potato gene.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied/source species for this gene is explicitly Ipomoea batatas.

Omics_Data.reason: PRJNA647243 is explicitly the 454 RNA-seq library, and proteomics by UHPLC-MS/MS was performed, but this omics information was mainly used for IbICS identification, not clearly for IbHCT specifically.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: IbHCT coding sequence was amplified from RNA extracted from I. batatas roots, and roots under nitrogen-free conditions were used in related experiments.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper says IbHCT was amplified from RNA extracted from I. batatas roots cultivated in nitrogen-free medium.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results and Discussion describe high 3,5-DiCQA accumulation in sweet potato roots; tubers were also used for enzyme-related purification work.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results state nitrogen depletion increased root 3,5-DiCQA, and IbHCT assays showed 5-CQA synthesis at pH 7 and 3,5-DiCQA formation at pH 6.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly concludes IbHCT low efficiency is insufficient to explain high 3,5-DiCQA accumulation, so it was not established as the key control step.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: IbHCT is shown to catalyze 5-CQA synthesis and also 3,5-DiCQA formation from 5-CQA plus caffeoyl-CoA, matching a midstream and downstream/final-step role.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results directly show in vitro formation of 3,5-DiCQA and 5-CQA by recombinant IbHCT.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: 3,5-DiCQA is the target metabolite directly formed by IbHCT in vitro.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: IbHCT is explicitly described as a BAHD acyltransferase containing the characteristic HXXXD and DFGWG motifs.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results support synthesis of 5-CQA from caffeoyl-CoA and quinic acid, 3,5-DiCQA from 5-CQA plus caffeoyl-CoA, and activity with cinnamoyl/p-coumaroyl/caffeoyl donors and quinate/shikimate acceptors.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The reported assays for IbHCT used caffeoyl-CoA with quinic acid and 5-CQA with caffeoyl-CoA; these substrates are explicitly tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state P3 was identified as 3,4-DiCQA likely from isomerization of 3,5-DiCQA, and IbHQT showed only slight p-coumaroylquinic acid activity and no DiCQA production.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports weak DiCQA-synthesis activity of IbHCT, but proposed engineering value 'in combination with IbICS or for precursor supply' is an extrapolation not directly tested.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Introduction lists antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antihepatotoxic, neuroprotective, antiviral, and ACE-inhibitory/hypotensive activities for 3,5-DiCQA.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: IbHCT is placed in the chlorogenic acid / 3,5-DiCQA branch of phenylpropanoid metabolism (Fig. 1 and text).

Primary_Product.reason: IbHCT forms 5-CQA efficiently and also 3,5-DiCQA in vitro.

Enzyme_Name.reason: IbHCT is explicitly expanded as hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA: shikimate/quinate hydroxycinnamoyl transferase.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: 3,5-DiCQA is described among phenolic compounds/phenolic acids in the paper.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: This description matches the paper's chlorogenic acid / dicaffeoylquinic acid branch within phenylpropanoid metabolism.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show IbHCT catalyzes 5-CQA formation and only low/trace 3,5-DiCQA synthesis in vitro.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper reports cloning, E. coli expression, purified enzyme assays, and HPLC/UV/MS identification of products.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly reflected in the Results and Discussion: cloning as BAHD ortholog, strong 5-CQA synthesis, and low-efficiency 3,5-DiCQA synthesis insufficient to explain in planta levels.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results and Data availability explicitly give the IbICS CDS GenBank ID as MT291823.

Source_Species.reason: IbICS was identified from sweet potato tissues.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is explicitly Ipomoea batatas.

Applied_Species.reason: IbICS was studied in sweet potato, transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana, and stably expressed in Pichia pastoris.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: All three names appear in the paper: Ipomoea batatas, Nicotiana benthamiana, and Pichia pastoris.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The vectors pEAQ-HT-DEST1 and pPICZαA are stated, and methanol induction in Pichia is described, but the exact promoter identities/strength ('strong constitutive', 'AOX1') are not explicitly named in the paper text provided.

Omics_Data.reason: IbICS was identified using the 454-based RNA-seq library PRJNA647243 and proteomic UHPLC-MS/MS peptide analysis; no proteomics accession is given.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Protein activity was enriched from tuber discs incubated 48 h in the dark, and transcript identification used roots from nitrogen-deprived plants.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper ties IbICS identification to nitrogen-deprived roots and induced tuber discs showing activity.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: 3,5-DiCQA accumulation is described in sweet potato roots, and tubers/discs were used for enzyme activity purification.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Nitrogen deprivation doubled 3,5-DiCQA in roots, dark incubation of tuber discs was used to induce activity, methanol induced expression in Pichia, and >10 mM 5-CQA caused inhibition.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion states IbICS 'could be the main actor in the synthesis of 3,5-DiCQA in sweet potato,' while also noting additional enzymes may contribute.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: IbICS is described as involved in the final/terminal step of 3,5-DiCQA synthesis from 5-CQA.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: IbICS directly converts 5-CQA into 3,5-DiCQA in assays and bioconversion experiments.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The terminal product linked to IbICS is explicitly 3,5-DiCQA.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: IbICS is explicitly described as a GDSL lipase-like protein, subclass SGNH hydrolase, with predicted N-terminal signal peptide and GDSL motif.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results show conversion of 5-CQA to 3,5-DiCQA, and the enzyme is named isochlorogenate synthase, consistent with chlorogenate-dependent transfer/disproportionation-like activity.

Primary_Substrate.reason: 5-CQA was the tested substrate that yielded 3,5-DiCQA.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly reports inactivity in E. coli and S. cerevisiae, activity in N. benthamiana and P. pastoris, no activity with other tested substrates, and discrepancies with the earlier 25-kDa enzyme suggesting additional enzymes.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Industrial bioproduction value in Pichia is directly supported, but broader breeding claims and combining with upstream genes/optimization strategies are partly inferential or proposed rather than demonstrated.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The Introduction explicitly attributes antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, antihepatotoxic, neuroprotective, antiviral, and ACE-inhibitory/hypotensive properties to 3,5-DiCQA.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: IbICS is placed in chlorogenic acid-derived 3,5-DiCQA biosynthesis within phenylpropanoid metabolism.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names IbICS as isochlorogenate synthase.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: 3,5-DiCQA is treated as a phenolic acid / chlorogenic acid derivative in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper directly reports inhibition at higher substrate concentrations (>10 mM 5-CQA) and does not report broader plant-level tradeoffs.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: This matches the terminal conversion from chlorogenic acid to dicaffeoylquinic acid described in the study.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show efficient 3,5-DiCQA production from 5-CQA and high-yield bioconversion in Pichia pastoris.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: In silico analysis predicted targeting to the secretory pathway, and in Pichia the protein was detected in the culture medium, consistent with secretion.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: IbICS was identified by proteomic/transcriptomic work, cloned, expressed in N. benthamiana, assayed by UHPLC-MS, kinetically characterized, and used in Pichia bioconversion.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The summary accurately reflects the results: identification by activity-guided proteomics plus RNA-seq, GDSL/SGNH identity and conversion of 5-CQA to 3,5-DiCQA, and >60% conversion in Pichia supporting a major final-step role.
